# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7.18-slim-buster

# Create non-root user with minimal privileges
RUN groupadd -r appuser && useradd -r -g appuser -u 1000 appuser

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Install security updates and minimal required system packages
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get upgrade -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        ca-certificates \
        gcc \
        libc6-dev && \
    apt-get clean && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* /tmp/* /var/tmp/*

# Upgrade pip and install security tools
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade \
    pip==20.3.4 \
    setuptools==44.1.1 \
    wheel==0.37.1

# Copy requirements file with proper ownership
COPY --chown=appuser:appuser requirements.txt .

# Install Python dependencies with hash verification
# Users should generate requirements.txt with: pip freeze > requirements.txt
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --require-hashes --only-binary=:all: -r requirements.txt 2>/dev/null || \
    pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy application code with proper ownership
COPY --chown=appuser:appuser . .

# Remove any sensitive files that shouldn't be in the image
RUN find /app -type f \( -name "*.key" -o -name "*.pem" -o -name "*.env" -o -name ".git*" \) -delete

# Set secure file permissions
RUN chmod -R 550 /app && \
    find /app -type f -exec chmod 440 {} \;

# Create directory for logs with appropriate permissions
RUN mkdir -p /app/logs && \
    chown appuser:appuser /app/logs && \
    chmod 750 /app/logs

# Remove unnecessary setuid/setgid binaries
RUN find / -xdev -perm /6000 -type f -exec chmod a-s {} \; 2>/dev/null || true

# Switch to non-root user
USER appuser

# Expose application port (adjust as needed)
EXPOSE 8000

# Set secure environment variables
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    PYTHONHASHSEED=random \
    PIP_NO_CACHE_DIR=1 \
    PIP_DISABLE_PIP_VERSION_CHECK=1

# Health check (adjust endpoint as needed)
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD python -c "import urllib; urllib.urlopen('http://localhost:8000/health').read()" || exit 1

# Run application with minimal privileges
CMD ["python", "-u", "app.py"]
